title: apera spica-venti subsp. interrupta
intro: Apera spica-venti subsp. interrupta, also known as the interrupted meadow grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Apera spica-venti subsp. interrupta include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
